name: Chicken Pot Pie Nouveau
recipeIngredient: 2 cups cooked chicken, cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 1 cup of broth to a boil. Add carrots and rosemary; cook for 4 minutes.
2. Drain the vegetables, reserving the liquid, then discard the rosemary.
3. Blanch sugar snap peas in boiling water for about 30 seconds, drain, rinse under cold water, and add to the carrots.
4. Make a small "X" at the root end of each onion; boil them for 10 minutes, drain, rinse, peel, and set aside.
5. In a saucepan, melt butter over medium-low heat. Add flour and cook, whisking, for 3–4 minutes.
6. Gradually add the reserved vegetable liquid and enough broth to make 2 cups. Cook, whisking, until thickened; stir in a sprig of rosemary. Cover and let cool.
7. Butter four 1-quart ramekins or oven-safe dishes. Arrange a layer of chicken in each dish, followed by vegetables, then pour the sauce over the mixture. Cover and refrigerate.
8. On a floured surface, roll out the puff pastry to a rectangle about ¼-inch thick; cut into 4 rectangles.
9. In a bowl, whisk egg and water together. Brush the rims of the dishes with this mixture, cover each with puff pastry, sealing edges.
10. Refrigerate the dishes for 20 minutes before preheating the oven to 475°F (245°C).
11. Bake until the pastry is puffed and golden, about 15–18 minutes.
12. Serves 4.
(adapted from The New Basics Cookbook)
